next_pass.focus: Experimental Validation of Entanglement Transition in Photonic Systems

open_gaps: Synthetic dimension coherence budget formalization - Is there an explicit 'coherence budget' equation for synthetic dimensions analogous to power budget in electronic design?
Entanglement transition experimental validation in photonic systems - Has area-law → volume-law transition been observed in photonic reservoir computing or photonic quantum systems specifically?
LSU finding replication and applications - Have other groups replicated the quantum coherence in classical light finding? What commercial or research applications are being developed?

passes.focus: Broad landscape survey - market, players, trends, coherence as resource
key_findings: Photonic computing market experiencing 20-30% CAGR, driven by AI accelerator demand
Key players: Lightmatter, Lightelligence, Celestial AI, Ayar Labs raised ~$1.47B+ combined
Coherence IS explicitly managed in photonic processors (MZI meshes, coherent detection)
CRITICAL: Partial coherence enhances parallelization (Nature 2024) - challenges 'more coherence = better' assumption
Coherence budget implicit in design (phase noise, crosstalk, thermal management)
No formal coherence-computation theorems found in survey
Photonic reservoir computing coherence scaling not found

passes.focus: Technical deep dive - formal theorems, partial coherence mechanism, RC scaling, coherence budgets
key_findings: Formal Theorems: Reck (1994) and Clements (2016) prove universality for coherent MZI meshes.
Partial Coherence Mechanism: Nature 2024 shows it suppresses phase noise and allows intensity-based parallelization (MAC operations).
Reservoir Computing Scaling: High coherence can reduce memory capacity due to noise and state-space crowding. Incoherent/Partially coherent systems scale more linearly with N.
Coherence Budget: Engineering reality known as 'Phase Noise Budget.' Includes PDK parameter 'Phase Coherence Length' (L_coh).
Coherence Threshold: Beyond L_coh, phase-sensitive computation is impossible without massive active compensation.

passes.focus: Synthesis — Deep Dive into Thresholds, Boundaries, and Costs
key_findings: Non-linearity thresholds reached milliwatt levels (143 mW) enabling All-Optical Activation Functions (NAFs).
Quantum-to-classical boundary defined by noise/loss thresholds (<2 dB/m waveguide loss, 99%+ detector efficiency).
LSU 2024 discovery: Quantum coherence isolated in classical light fields using PNR detection, blurring ontological boundaries.
Active thermal tuning has 1-20 mW static power floor; passive PCMs (Sb₂Se₃) have zero static power, enabling true aJ-level efficiency.
Photonic Reservoir Computing: Incoherent systems have higher memory capacity; coherent systems have higher compute efficiency due to passive interference math.

passes.focus: Synthetic Dimensions and Entanglement Phase Transitions in Propagation Media
key_findings: Synthetic dimensions achieve O(1) component scaling vs O(N²) spatial; coherence must span full frequency lattice (edge-to-edge)
Demonstrated: 25×25 to 50×50 MVM in single modulator; throughput scales to tens of POPS with WDM
Entanglement area→volume law transition driven by quasiparticle lifetime under measurement (coherence length analog)
Two systems found: Sauter-Schwinger effect (field strength driven) and monitored fermion chains (measurement rate driven)
LSU finding confirmed: PNR + OAM measurements isolate quantum coherence from classical thermal light
Quantum-classical boundary is measurement-dependent, not ontological — supports propagation framework coherence spectrum view
